rulings.Case Title: MOSES JAVAHERI VS. STEPHAN BAGNESCHI et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendant Stephan Bagneschi'S Motion To Compel Deposition
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, November 18, 2013, Line 7, Defendant Stephan Bagneschi'S Motion To Compel Deposition. Pro Tem Judge Tom Paoli, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Granted. $200 sanctions to be paid by plaintiff to defendant within 30 days. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to tompaoli9@gmail.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: CHARLES E. YEAGER et al VS. WILD, CARTER & TIPTON et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
rulings.Rulings: On calendar for Monday, November 18, 2013, Line 4, DEFENDANTs DE LA PENA & MCDONALD, LLP, STEPHEN MCDONALD and CHARLES MURRAY's MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT The motion is granted with respect to the Bowlin and AMD matters. Defendants have submitted new evidence meeting their burden of production, and Plaintiffs have submitted no evidence raising an issue of fact as to whether there was a republication such that the statute of limitations had not run. The motion is granted as to the AT&T matter. Defendants submitted new evidence as to the lack of any impact on the judgment, and Plaintiffs made no substantive response to those arguments or evidence. The possibility of excessive fees or mishandled documents is not a basis to deny the motion in the absence of evidence that there would have been a more advantageous outcome. The motion is denied with respect to the homeowners' association action. A plaintiff's statements at deposition are an inadequate basis to prove the absence of any evidence to assert a claim. Just because Mrs. Yeager is not aware of claims or evidence, does not establish that Plaintiffs' counsel will not be able to produce evidence at trial. Defendants have not carried their burden of production. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and in compliance with Code of Civil Procedure 437c(g) and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/pjb

rulings.Case Title: CHARLES E. YEAGER et al VS. WILD, CARTER & TIPTON et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
rulings.Rulings: On calendar for Monday, November 18, 2013, Line 4, DEFENDANTs CHARLES MURRAY, MCDONALD, STEVEN E. (SUED AS STEPHEN E. MCDONALD) and DE LA PENA & MCDONALD, LLP's MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT The motion with respect to the Yeager v. Yeager matter is denied. The motion is an improper motion to reconsider that is based on no new evidence. Defendants were free to offer an expert opinion with their original motion, but did not. The expert opinion is based on the same evidence submitted with the original motion. In any event, if Defendants' expert opinion were to be considered, Plaintiffs' opposing expert would be sufficient to raise a triable issue of material fact for the trial judge as to how the underlying appeal would have been resolved. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and in compliance with Code of Civil Procedure 437c(g) and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/pjb

rulings.Case Title: BENTLY RESERVE, L.P., A CALIFORIA LIMITED et al VS. ANDREAS G. PAPALIOLIOS, AN INDIVIDUAL, AKA "SAL R" et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Plaintiffs Bently Nob Hill, L.P., And Christopher Bently'S Demurrer To Amended Answer To Complaint
rulings.Rulings: On calendar for Monday, November 18, 2013, Line 33, Plaintiffs Bently Nob Hill, L.P., And Christopher Bently'S Demurrer To Amended Answer To Complaint The demurrers to the 1st and 5th defenses are sustained without leave to amend. The complaint states a cause of action for libel including the necessary allegations. The demurrers to the 3rd and 7th defenses are overruled. It is for the trier of fact to determine the viability of this defense as to the various allegations. The Court of Appeal did not make any binding findings that the defense fails as a matter of law as to all alleged libels. The demurrers to the 4th, 6th, 10th, and 13th defenses are overruled because they are legal defenses based on the allegations of the complaint or adequately state the necessary facts. The demurrers to the remaining defenses are sustained with 30 days leave to amend. The defenses are totally conclusory and do not meet the pleading requirements of FPI Dev. Inc. v. Nakashima, (1991) 231 Cal.App.3d 367, 384. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/pjb

rulings.Case Title: EDEN GONZALEA HASS et al VS. RHODYCO PRODUCTIONS et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Determination Of A Good Faith Settlement
rulings.Rulings: On calendar for Monday, November 18, 2013, Line 11, DEFENDANTS KAISER FOUNDATION HEALTH PLANS, INC., KAISER FOUNDATION HOSPITALS AND THE PERMANENTE MEDICAL GROUP, INCORPROATED'S Motion For Determination Of A Good Faith Settlement Defendants Kaiser Foundation Health Plans, Inc. Kaiser Foundation Hospitals, and The Permanente Medical Group, Inc.'s motion for determination of a good faith settlement is granted. All requests for judicial notice are granted. Defendants have produced substantial evidence demonstrating the amount of the settlement is within the reasonable range of its proportional share of comparative liability for Plaintiff's injuries. (See Tech-Bilt Inc. v. Woodward Clyde & Assoc. (1985) 38 Cal.3d 488, 499.) The Tech-Bilt factors weigh in favor of Defendants. Defendant Palmer College of Chiropractic-West has not carried its burden of showing the settlement amount is so far out of the ballpark that it is inconsistent with the equitable goals of the statute. (See CCP §877.6(d); Tech-Bilt, supra, 38 Cal.3d at 500.) Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/PJB

The prayer for punitive damages as to Defendant GNC is stricken, with 10 days leave to amend. Plaintiff has not pled the requisite facts to assert a punitive damages claim against Defendant GNC, a corporate defendant. (Civ. Code § 3294(b).) The motion is otherwise denied. Plaintiff has adequately alleged facts supporting the allegation that Defendant Vilhauer's alleged defamatory statements were fraudulent. rulings.Case Title: ASGHAR (VAHEED) TAHERI VS. ESREF TEKER et al

rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, November 18, 2013, Line 6, PLAINTIFF ASGHAR TAHERI's Motion To Discovery. Pro Tem Judge Tom Paoli, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Motion to compel further responses to demand for production of documents granted. Plaintiff shall provide within 30 days further responses identifying all responsive documents and produce such documents. If Plaintiff does not possess responsive documents, plaintiff shall state whether such documents were ever in plaintiff's possession, and if so, what happened to them. To the extent plaintiff raises privacy objections or claims a privilege, plaintiff shall produce a privilege log identifying generally each document and the basis for the objection or claim. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to tompaoli9@gmail.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. rulings.Case Title: CHRISTINA NICHOLAS VS. GREENE MOTORS, INC. DBA AVERY GREENE HONDA et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And M9otion To Dismiss Of Transfer Action For Improper Venue; Request For Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday, November 18, 2013, Line 29: Defendant GREENE MOTORS, INC. dba AVERY GREENE HONDA'S Motion To Dismiss Of Transfer Action For Improper Venue; Request For Sanctions Denied. A defendant is a sham defendant if it appears from the complaint that a cause of action cannot be stated against that defendant. (Konnoff v. Fraser (1944) 62 Cal.App.2d 788, 791-92.) Here, the complaint pleads a cause of action under the Consumers Legal Remedy Act against Defendant Honda. It is true that the main allegations are principally based on allegations of misrepresentations by Greene Motors. The complaint, however, liberally construed as the Court is required to do, also alleges that it was represented to Plaintiff that she was purchasing a Honda Certified Used Car. (Complaint, 12.) This arguably passed on to Plaintiff a representation by Honda itself that, by certifying the used car, Honda represented that the car had been thoroughly inspected and met certain standards. (Id.) The complaint alleges that Civ. Code § 1770(a)(5) was violated because the car is unsafe to operate on a roadway, which directly contradicts the representations made by Honda through its certification. (Id., 22.) So construed, the facts pled sufficiently state a cause of action against Honda. Therefore, Honda is not a sham defendant and may be considered for venue purposes. Under Civ. Code, § 1780(d)), venue is proper in the county in which the defendant "is doing business." Defendant Greene Motors, Inc. has not met its burden in establishing that Honda does not do business in San Francisco. Because venue is proper in San Francisco for Defendant Honda, venue is proper in San Francisco for all defendants. (Monogram Co. of Cal. v. Kingsley (1951) 38 Cal.2d 28, 29-30.) Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/PJB

rulings.Case Title: RAY P. SIOTTO et al VS. ROCK AND ROSE LANDSCAPING & BIRKMYER ASSOCIATES, et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday, November 18, 2013, Line 30: Defendant ROCK AND ROSE LANDSCAPING & BIRKMYER ASSOCIATES' DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT The demurrer to the third cause of action for fraudulent inducement is overruled. The complaint pleads sufficient specific facts to support the claim. The demurrer to the negligent infliction of emotional distress cause of action is sustained without leave to amend. First, it is not an independent tort. See Potter v. Firestone Tire & Rubber Co. (1993) 6 Cal.4th 965, 984. Second, under Erlich v. Menezes (1999) 21 Cal.4th 543, 553-554, intentional misconduct is necessary to recover tort damages. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/PJB

rulings.Case Title: RAY P. SIOTTO et al VS. ROCK AND ROSE LANDSCAPING & BIRKMYER ASSOCIATES, et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday, November 18, 2013, Line 30: Defendant ROCK AND ROSE LANDSCAPING & BIRKMYER ASSOCIATES' MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT The Court denies the motion to strike. The third and fourth causes of action support the prayer for exemplary damages. The intentional tort claims may support the recovery of emotional distress damages under Erlich v. Menezes (1999) 21 Cal.4th 543, 553-554.
